title: Friday Sermon 18 October 2002
description:
O ye who believe!  Turn to Allah in sincere repentance.  It may be that your Lord will cancel the evil of your deeds and let you enter Gardens with flowing streams, on the day when Allah will not abase the Prophet nor those who have believed alone with him.  With their light streaming out before them and on their right hands so they will say 'Our Lord perfect our light  for us and forgive us; surely Thou hast power over all that He wills.'

('The Holy Quran'.  Al Tahrim [The Prohibition]: 9).

The verse explicates the spiritual development of believers in this life and the continuation of it in the life after death, in terms of the desire of the believers to be forgiven and to acquire and further excel in light.

The Holy Prophet (peace and blessings be on him) said that on the Day of Judgment he would recognise and identify his Ummah (people) for their books of deeds would be handed to them in their right hands and there would be Light running before them.*

The Promised Messiah (on whom be peace) further explained this and wrote that people who possessed the light of faith in this world would be as if immersed in it on the Day of Judgment and they would be beseeching their Lord to make this light reach the point of excellence:

… The light of the believers will run before them and on their right hands.  They will supplicate: Lord, perfect our light for us and cover us with Thy grace, surely Thou hast power over all things.  Their supplication that their light may be perfected is an indication of limitless progress.  It means that when they have arrived at one stage of illumination, they will perceive a higher stage ahead of them and viewing it they will consider the stage in which they are as being inferior and will supplicate for the attainment of the higher stage, and when they arrive at that stage they will perceive a still higher third stage ahead of them and they will supplicate for its achievement.  Thus their yearning for constant progress is indicated by the expression: Do Thou perfect our light.  In short, this chain of progress will continue indefinitely.  There will be no falling away, nor will they be expelled from heaven, but will daily advance further forward.
  
Thus the dwellers of heaven will seek the attainment of perfection and their complete absorption in light.

(Hazrat Mirza Ghulam Ahmad.  'Islami Usul Ki Philosophy' ['The Philosophy of the Teachings of Islam'].  Matba Siddiqi, 1896, published 1905.  'Ruhani Khazain: x'. 412, 413.  English: Islam International Publications Ltd, 2010. 155, 156).

* Ahmad ibn Hanbal.  'Musnad Ahmad ibn Hanbal'.

The text https://www.alislam.org/urdu/sermon/FST20021018-UR.pdf
